Deps live only in the project venv, which is git-ignored and must be recreated after a rebuild. Running `python3 -m uvicorn ...` (the old command) fails with `No module named uvicorn`. Always invoke `.venv/bin/python` (or activate the venv) instead.\n11\t\n12\t### One-time setup (or after a NixOS rebuild)\n13\t\n14\t```bash\n15\t# From /home/coding/aide-de-camp/\n16\tpython3 -m venv .venv\n17\t.venv/bin/pip install -e \".[dev]\"\n18\t```\n19\t\n20\tThis installs fastapi, `uvicorn[standard]`, httpx, websockets, pydantic, aiosqlite (runtime) plus pytest, pytest-asyncio, ruff (dev), and the `src` package itself in editable mode, all from `pyproject.toml`.\n21\t\n22\tVerify it took:\n23\t\n24\t```bash\n25\t.venv/bin/python -c \"import fastapi, uvicorn, httpx, pydantic, aiosqlite; print('deps OK')\"\n26\t.venv/bin/pytest --collect-only -q   # should collect ~60+ tests\n27\t```\n28\t\n29\t### Start / restart\n30\t\n31\t```bash\n32\t# From /home/coding/aide-de-camp/\n33\tnohup .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\n34\t\n35\t# Restart (kill then start)\n36\tkill -2 $(ps aux | grep \"uvicorn src.main\" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}') 2>/dev/null; true\n37\tnohup .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\n38\t\n39\t# Health check (expect HTTP 200 + {\"status\":\"ok\",\"service\":\"adc-voice\"})\n40\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\n41\t```\n42\t\n43\tLogs go to `/tmp/adc.log`. Root logging is configured in `src/main.py` — all `src.*` module loggers are visible at INFO level.\n44\t\n45\t## Versioning\n46\t\n47\t**Every meaningful push must bump the version and cut a git tag.**\n48\t\n49\tVersion lives in `pyproject.toml` → `[project] version`. At startup, `src/main.py` reads it via `tomllib` and passes it to FastAPI — the canvas header badge reads from `/openapi.json` which reflects this. **Do not hardcode the version anywhere else.**\n50\t\n51\t### Scheme: semantic versioning (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`)\n52\t\n53\t- `PATCH` — bug fixes, pipeline corrections, prompt tweaks\n54\t- `MINOR` — new features, new intent types, new fetch sources, UI additions\n55\t- `MAJOR` — architectural changes, breaking API changes\n56\t\n57\t### Release checklist\n58\t\n59\t1. Edit `pyproject.toml` — bump `version`\n60\t2. Commit: `git commit -m \"chore: bump to vX.Y.Z\"`\n61\t3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`\n62\t4. Push: `git push origin main --tags`\n63\t\n64\tThere is no CI build for adc — it runs directly from source. The tag is the release.\n65\t\n66\t## Architecture\n67\t\n68\t```\n69\tutterance (text or voice)\n70\t  → POST /dispatch\n71\t  → intent router (ZAI LLM classify)\n72\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\n73\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\n74\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(event_type=\"result_created\", target_surface_id=...)\n75\t  → canvas SSE listener calls loadTopics()\n76\t  → GET /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics → cards rendered\n77\t```\n78\t\n79\tKey files:\n80\t- `src/main.py` — FastAPI app, `/dispatch` endpoint, SSE broadcaster wiring\n81\t- `src/intent/router.py` — LLM classification → fetch+synthesize → store persistence\n82\t- `src/fetch/commands.py` — fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures\n83\t- `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` — concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation)\n84\t- `src/synthesize/strand.py` — LLM synthesis into structured result\n85\t- `src/session/store.py` — SQLite session store (aiosqlite); `data/session.db`\n86\t- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` — SSE connection registry and event routing\n87\t- `src/canvas/index.html` — single-page canvas UI\n88\t\n89\t## ZAI Proxy\n90\t\n91\tAll LLM calls go through the ZAI proxy at:\n92\t\n93\t```\n94\thttps://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages\n95\t```\n96\t\n97\tSet via `ZAI_PROXY_URL` env var; default is the above. The vpn-wildcard-tls cert on the Traefik entrypoint is self-signed from Traefik's perspective — all httpx clients must use `verify=False`.\n98\t\n99\t**GLM-4.7 wraps all JSON responses in ` ```json ... ``` ` markdown fences.** Strip them before `json.loads()`:\n100\t\n101\t```python\n102\traw = response.strip()\n103\tif raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n104\t    raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n105\t    raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n106\tresult = json.loads(raw)\n107\t```\n108\t\n109\tThe proxy also wraps the Anthropic response envelope under a `\"result\"` key. Unwrap with `data.get(\"result\", data)` before accessing `content`/`usage`/`model`.\n110\t\n111\t## Session Store\n112\t\n113\t- `get_store()` is **synchronous** — do not `await` it\n114\t- `find_or_create_topic()` returns `(topic_id: str, created: bool)` — always unpack\n115\t- Topic `type` must be one of `('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')` — map intent types before passing\n116\t- `create_utterance(session_id, raw_text, utterance_id=None)` — `utterance_id` is optional\n117\t\n118\t## SSE Broadcaster\n119\t\n120\tCanvas listens for `\"result_created\"` and `\"topic_updated\"` events. Broadcast with:\n121\t\n122\t```python\n123\tfrom src.sse import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent\n124\t\n125\tbroadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n126\tawait broadcaster.broadcast(\n127\t    SSEEvent(\n128\t        event_type=\"result_created\",\n129\t        target_surface_id=surface_id,\n130\t        data={...}\n131\t    )\n132\t)\n133\t```\n134\t\n135\tSSEEvent supports optional targeting filters:\n136\t- `target_session_id` — only send to connections for this session\n137\t- `target_surface_id` — only send to this specific surface\n138\t- `exclude_surface_id` — send to all surfaces except this one\n139\t\n140\t## Canvas Dispatch Contract\n141\t\n142\tThe canvas sends `surface_id` with every dispatch POST — required for SSE targeting:\n143\t\n144\t```javascript\n145\tbody: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId })\n146\t```\n147\t\n148\tThe canvas fetches topics from `/api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics` on SSE `result_created`.\n149\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_ac91dbc433dc4ca3bb197c06","session_id":"f403b630-d9eb-4661-a4ec-b403327c9a83","uuid":"cc2d46eb-46c4-48ea-b94b-9a269e9614e0","timestamp":"2026-07-24T13:39:19.295Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore codebase for pipeline and MCP logs"}

CRITICAL - Active production issue  \n361\t**Impact:** Eliminates primary failure mode  \n362\t\n363\t**Recommended Code Solution:**\n364\t```python\n365\tdef calculate_iv(chunk):\n366\t    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with proper input validation\"\"\"\n367\t    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n368\t        t = row['T']  # Time to expiration\n369\t        F = row['F']  # Forward price  \n370\t        K = row['K']  # Strike price\n371\t        price = row['undiscounted_option_price']\n372\t        \n373\t        # Pre-calculation validation\n374\t        if t <= 0:\n375\t            logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n376\t            continue\n377\t        if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n378\t            logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n379\t            continue\n380\t        if price <= 0:\n381\t            logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n382\t            continue\n383\t        \n384\t        # Safe calculation with exception handling\n385\t        try:\n386\t            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n387\t                price, F, K, t, flag\n388\t            )\n389\t        except (ZeroDivisionError, ValueError) as e:\n390\t            logger.error(f\"Calculation failed for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n391\t            continue\n392\t        \n393\t        chunk.at[idx, 'IV'] = iv\n394\t    return chunk\n395\t```\n396\t\n397\t**Deployment Steps:**\n398\t1. Update calculation code in options pipeline\n399\t2. Add comprehensive input validation\n400\t3. Implement graceful error handling\n401\t4. Add monitoring for validation failures\n402\t5. days\n419\t- Pod restart count: Stabilized (no increase)\n420\t- Validation warnings: Logged for invalid data rejection\n421\t\n422\t### Medium-Term Actions 🟡\n423\t\n424\t#### 3. **Implement Data Quality Validation Layer**\n425\t\n426\t**Priority:** HIGH - Prevents invalid data from reaching calculations\n427\t\n428\t**Recommended Architecture:**\n429\t```python\n430\tclass OptionsDataValidator:\n431\t    \"\"\"Validate options data before expensive calculations\"\"\"\n432\t    \n433\t    def __init__(self):\n434\t        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n435\t    \n436\t    def validate_row(self, row):\n437\t        \"\"\"Validate a single options data row\"\"\"\n438\t        checks = [\n439\t            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']}\"),\n440\t            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']}\"),\n441\t            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']}\"),\n442\t            (row['undiscounted_option_price'] > 0, f\"Invalid price={row['undiscounted_option_price']}\")\n443\t        ]\n444\t        \n445\t        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n446\t            if not valid:\n447\t                self.logger.warning(f\"Data validation failed: {error_msg} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}\")\n448\t                return False\n449\t        return True\n450\t    \n451\t    def filter_chunk(self, chunk):\n452\t        \"\"\"Filter out invalid rows from a chunk\"\"\"\n453\t        valid_rows = []\n454\t        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n455\t            if self.validate_row(row):\n456\t                valid_rows.append(row)\n457\t        return valid_rows\n458\t```\n459\t\n460\t**Integration Point:** Pre-calculation in the processing pipeline\n461\t\n462\t#### 4. **Add Telemetry for Logging:**\n496\t```python\n497\timport json\n498\timport logging\n499\t\n500\tclass StructuredLogger:\n501\t    def log_event(self, level, event_type, **kwargs):\n502\t        log_entry = {\n503\t            'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),\n504\t            'level': level,\n505\t            'event': event_type,\n506\t            **kwargs\n507\t        }\n508\t health visualization\n514\t\n515\t#### 6. **Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern**\n516\t\n517\t**Architecture:**\n518\t```python\n519\tclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n520\t    \"\"\"Prevent cascade failures by stopping calculations after threshold\"\"\"\n521\t    \n522\t    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n523\t        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n524\t        self.timeout = timeout\n525\t        self.failures = 0\n526\t        self.last_failure_time = None\n527\t        self.state = 'CLOSED'  # CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN\n528\t    \n529\t    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n530\t        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n531\t        if self.state == 'OPEN':\n532\t            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n533\t                self.state = 'HALF_OPEN'\n534\t            else:\n535\t                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN - too many recent failures\")\n536\t        \n537\t        try:\n538\t            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n539\t            if self.state == 'HALF_OPEN':\n540\t                self.state = 'CLOSED'\n541\t                self.failures = 0\n542\t            return result\n543\t        except ZeroDivisionError:\n544\t            self.failures += 1\n545\t            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n546\t            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n547\t                self.state = 'OPEN'\n548\t            raise\n549\t```\n550\t\n551\t**Configuration:** 10 failures → 5-minute cooldown period\n552\t\n553\t#### 7. **IBKR MCP Historical Pod Cleanup**\n554\t\n555\t**Priority:** LOW - Housekeeping only\n556\t\n557\t**Action:**\n558\t```bash\n559\t# Delete failed **Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options Pipeline**\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL - Active production issue  \n**Impact:** Eliminates primary failure mode  \n\n**Recommended Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with proper input validation\"\"\"\n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        t = row['T']  # Time to expiration\n        F = row['F']  # Forward price  \n        K = row['K']  # Strike price\n        price = row['undiscounted_option_price']\n        \n        # Pre-calculation validation\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if price <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        \n        # Safe calculation with exception handling\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                price, F, K, t, flag\n            )\n        except (ZeroDivisionError, ValueError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failed for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n            continue\n        \n        chunk.at[idx, 'IV'] = iv\n    return chunk\n```\n\n**Deployment Steps:**\n1. calculations\n\n**Recommended Architecture:**\n```python\nclass OptionsDataValidator:\n    \"\"\"Validate options data before expensive calculations\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n    \n    def validate_row(self, row):\n        \"\"\"Validate a single options data row\"\"\"\n        checks = [\n            (row['T'] > 0, f\"Invalid T={row['T']}\"),\n            (row['F'] > 0, f\"Invalid F={row['F']}\"),\n            (row['K'] > 0, f\"Invalid K={row['K']}\"),\n            (row['undiscounted_option_price'] > 0, f\"Invalid price={row['undiscounted_option_price']}\")\n        ]\n        \n        for valid, error_msg in checks:\n            if not valid:\n                self.logger.warning(f\"Data validation failed: {error_msg} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}\")\n                return False\n        return True\n    \n    def filter_chunk(self, chunk):\n        \"\"\"Filter out invalid rows from a chunk\"\"\"\n        valid_rows = []\n        for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n            if self.validate_row(row):\n                valid_rows.append(row)\n        return valid_rows\n```\n\n**Integration Point:** Pre-calculation in the processing pipeline\n\n#### 4. **Add Telemetry for Data Quality**\n\n**Prometheus Metrics:**\n```python\nfrom prometheus_client import Counter, # t_zero, f_invalid, k_invalid, price_invalid\n    ),\n    'options_calculation_success_total': Counter(\n        'options_calculation_success_total',\n        'Successful options calculations'\n    ),\n    'options_calculation_duration_seconds': Histogram(\n        'options_calculation_duration_seconds',\n        'Options calculation duration'\n    )\n}\n```\n\n**Monitoring Dashboard:** Grafana visualization\n\n#### 6. **Implement Circuit Breaker Pattern**\n\n**Architecture:**\n```python\nclass OptionsCalculationCircuitBreaker:\n    \"\"\"Prevent cascade failures by stopping calculations after threshold\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold=10, timeout=300):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout = timeout\n        self.failures = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = 'CLOSED'  # CLOSED, OPEN, HALF_OPEN\n    \n    def call(self, func, *args, **kwargs):\n        \"\"\"Execute function with circuit breaker protection\"\"\"\n        if self.state == 'OPEN':\n            if time.time() - self.last_failure_time > self.timeout:\n                self.state = 'HALF_OPEN'\n            else:\n                raise CircuitBreakerOpenError(\"Circuit breaker is OPEN - too many recent failures\")\n        \n        try:\n            result = func(*args, **kwargs)\n            if self.state == 'HALF_OPEN':\n                self.state = 'CLOSED'\n                self.failures = 0\n            return result\n        except ZeroDivisionError:\n            self.failures += 1\n            self.last_failure_time = time.time()\n            if self.failures >= self.failure_threshold:\n                self.state = 'OPEN'\n            raise\n```\n\n**Configuration:** 10 failures → 5-minute cooldown period\n\n#### 7. **IBKR MCP Historical Pod Cleanup**\n\n**Priority:** LOW - Housekeeping only\n\n**Action:**\n```bash\n# Delete failed historical pods\nkubectl --server=http://traefik-ardenone-cluster:8001 delete Edit `pyproject.toml` — bump `version`\\n60\\t2. Commit: `git commit -m \\\"chore: bump to vX.Y.Z\\\"`\\n61\\t3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`\\n62\\t4. aide-de-camp — Agent Instructions\\n2\\t\\n3\\t## What This Is\\n4\\t\\n5\\tFastAPI server providing a voice/text → intent routing → parallel fetch+synthesize → SSE canvas pipeline. Runs locally on the Hetzner server at port 8000.\\n6\\t\\n7\\t## Running the Server\\n8\\t\\n9\\t> **Use the repo venv (`.venv/bin/python`), never bare system `python3`.**\\n10\\t> This host runs NixOS. The system `python3` (`/run/current-system/sw/bin/python3`, 3.12) ships **no pip, fastapi, uvicorn, httpx, or pytest** — they are not Nix-managed, so a `nixos-rebuild` wipes them. Deps live only in the project venv, which is git-ignored and must be recreated after a rebuild. Running `python3 -m uvicorn ...` (the old command) fails with `No module named uvicorn`. Always invoke `.venv/bin/python` (or activate the venv) instead.\\n11\\t\\n12\\t### One-time setup (or after a NixOS rebuild)\\n13\\t\\n14\\t```bash\\n15\\t# From /home/coding/aide-de-camp/\\n16\\tpython3 -m venv .venv\\n17\\t.venv/bin/pip install -e \\\".[dev]\\\"\\n18\\t```\\n19\\t\\n20\\tThis installs fastapi, `uvicorn[standard]`, httpx, websockets, pydantic, aiosqlite (runtime) plus pytest, pytest-asyncio, ruff (dev), and the `src` package itself in editable mode, all from `pyproject.toml`.\\n21\\t\\n22\\tVerify it took:\\n23\\t\\n24\\t```bash\\n25\\t.venv/bin/python -c \\\"import fastapi, uvicorn, httpx, pydantic, aiosqlite; print('deps OK')\\\"\\n26\\t.venv/bin/pytest --collect-only -q   # should collect ~60+ tests\\n27\\t```\\n28\\t\\n29\\t### Start / restart\\n30\\t\\n31\\t```bash\\n32\\t# From /home/coding/aide-de-camp/\\n33\\tnohup .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\\n34\\t\\n35\\t# Restart (kill then start)\\n36\\tkill -2 $(ps aux | grep \\\"uvicorn src.main\\\" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}') 2>/dev/null; true\\n37\\tnohup .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\\n38\\t\\n39\\t# Health check (expect HTTP 200 + {\\\"status\\\":\\\"ok\\\",\\\"service\\\":\\\"adc-voice\\\"})\\n40\\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\\n41\\t```\\n42\\t\\n43\\tLogs go to `/tmp/adc.log`. Root logging is configured in `src/main.py` — all `src.*` module loggers are visible at INFO level.\\n44\\t\\n45\\t## Versioning\\n46\\t\\n47\\t**Every meaningful push must bump the version and cut a git tag.**\\n48\\t\\n49\\tVersion lives in `pyproject.toml` → `[project] version`. At startup, `src/main.py` reads it via `tomllib` and passes it to FastAPI — the canvas header badge reads from `/openapi.json` which reflects this. **Do not hardcode the version anywhere else.**\\n50\\t\\n51\\t### Scheme: semantic versioning (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`)\\n52\\t\\n53\\t- `PATCH` — bug fixes, pipeline corrections, prompt tweaks\\n54\\t- `MINOR` — new features, new intent types, new fetch sources, UI additions\\n55\\t- `MAJOR` — architectural changes, breaking API changes\\n56\\t\\n57\\t### Release checklist\\n58\\t\\n59\\t1. Edit `pyproject.toml` — bump `version`\\n60\\t2. Commit: `git commit -m \\\"chore: bump to vX.Y.Z\\\"`\\n61\\t3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`\\n62\\t4. Push: `git push origin main --tags`\\n63\\t\\n64\\tThere is no CI build for adc — it runs directly from source. The tag is the release.\\n65\\t\\n66\\t## Architecture\\n67\\t\\n68\\t```\\n69\\tutterance (text or voice)\\n70\\t  → POST /dispatch\\n71\\t  → intent router (ZAI LLM classify)\\n72\\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\\n73\\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\\n74\\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(event_type=\\\"result_created\\\", target_surface_id=...)\\n75\\t  → canvas SSE listener calls loadTopics()\\n76\\t  → GET /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics → cards rendered\\n77\\t```\\n78\\t\\n79\\tKey files:\\n80\\t- `src/main.py` — FastAPI app, `/dispatch` endpoint, SSE broadcaster wiring\\n81\\t- `src/intent/router.py` — LLM classification → fetch+synthesize → store persistence\\n82\\t- `src/fetch/commands.py` — fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures\\n83\\t- `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` — concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation)\\n84\\t- `src/synthesize/strand.py` — LLM synthesis into structured result\\n85\\t- `src/session/store.py` — SQLite session store (aiosqlite); `data/session.db`\\n86\\t- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` — SSE connection registry and event routing\\n87\\t- `src/canvas/index.html` — single-page canvas UI\\n88\\t\\n89\\t## ZAI Proxy\\n90\\t\\n91\\tAll LLM calls go through the ZAI proxy at:\\n92\\t\\n93\\t```\\n94\\thttps://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages\\n95\\t```\\n96\\t\\n97\\tSet via `ZAI_PROXY_URL` env var; default is the above. The vpn-wildcard-tls cert on the Traefik entrypoint is self-signed from Traefik's perspective — all httpx clients must use `verify=False`.\\n98\\t\\n99\\t**GLM-4.7 wraps all JSON responses in ` ```json ... ``` ` markdown fences.** Strip them before `json.loads()`:\\n100\\t\\n101\\t```python\\n102\\traw = response.strip()\\n103\\tif raw.startswith(\\\"```\\\"):\\n104\\t    raw = raw.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\", 1)[-1]\\n105\\t    raw = raw.rsplit(\\\"```\\\", 1)[0].strip()\\n106\\tresult = json.loads(raw)\\n107\\t```\\n108\\t\\n109\\tThe proxy also wraps the Anthropic response envelope under a `\\\"result\\\"` key. Unwrap with `data.get(\\\"result\\\", data)` before accessing `content`/`usage`/`model`.\\n110\\t\\n111\\t## Session Store\\n112\\t\\n113\\t- `get_store()` is **synchronous** — do not `await` it\\n114\\t- `find_or_create_topic()` returns `(topic_id: str, created: bool)` — always unpack\\n115\\t- Topic `type` must be one of `('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')` — map intent types before passing\\n116\\t- `create_utterance(session_id, raw_text, utterance_id=None)` — `utterance_id` is optional\\n117\\t\\n118\\t## SSE Broadcaster\\n119\\t\\n120\\tCanvas listens for `\\\"result_created\\\"` and `\\\"topic_updated\\\"` events. Broadcast with:\\n121\\t\\n122\\t```python\\n123\\tfrom src.sse import get_broadcaster, SSEEvent\\n124\\t\\n125\\tbroadcaster = get_broadcaster()\\n126\\tawait broadcaster.broadcast(\\n127\\t    SSEEvent(\\n128\\t        event_type=\\\"result_created\\\",\\n129\\t        target_surface_id=surface_id,\\n130\\t        data={...}\\n131\\t    )\\n132\\t)\\n133\\t```\\n134\\t\\n135\\tSSEEvent supports optional targeting filters:\\n136\\t- `target_session_id` — only send to connections for this session\\n137\\t- `target_surface_id` — only send to this specific surface\\n138\\t- `exclude_surface_id` — send to all surfaces except this one\\n139\\t\\n140\\t## Canvas Dispatch Contract\\n141\\t\\n142\\tThe canvas sends `surface_id` with every dispatch POST — required for SSE targeting:\\n143\\t\\n144\\t```javascript\\n145\\tbody: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId })\\n146\\t```\\n147\\t\\n148\\tThe canvas fetches topics from _validate_timeout_ms(value: Any, source_name: str) -> int | None:\\n87\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n88\\t    Validate a timeout_ms value.\\n89\\t\\n90\\t    Args:\\n91\\t        value: The timeout value to validate\\n92\\t        source_name: Name of the source for error messages\\n93\\t\\n94\\t    Returns:\\n95\\t        The validated timeout as int in seconds, or None if not specified\\n96\\t\\n97\\t    Raises:\\n98\\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If the timeout value is invalid\\n99\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n100\\t    if value is None:\\n101\\t        return None\\n102\\t\\n103\\t    # Must be an integer or float that can be converted to int\\n104\\t    if not isinstance(value, (int, float)):\\n105\\t        raise FetchConfigValidationError([\\n106\\t            f\\\"{source_name}: timeout_ms must be a number, got {type(value).__name__}\\\"\\n107\\t        ])\\n108\\t\\n109\\t    # Convert to seconds (timeout_ms is in milliseconds)\\n110\\t    timeout_seconds = int(value) / 1000\\n111\\t\\n112\\t    # Must be positive\\n113\\t    if timeout_seconds <= 0:\\n114\\t        raise FetchConfigValidationError([\\n115\\t            f\\\"{source_name}: timeout_ms must be positive, got {value}\\\"\\n116\\t        ])\\n117\\t\\n118\\t    return timeout_seconds\\n119\\t\\n120\\t\\n121\\tdef _load_fetch_config() -> dict:\\n122\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n123\\t    Load fetch configuration from YAML file.\\n124\\t\\n125\\t    Returns:\\n126\\t        Dictionary with source timeouts. Empty dict if file not found or invalid.\\n127\\t\\n128\\t    Raises:\\n129\\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If config schema is invalid\\n130\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n131\\t    global _fetch_config_cache, _fetch_config_mtime\\n132\\t\\n133\\t    # Check if we need to reload (file modified or not loaded yet)\\n134\\t    try:\\n135\\t        current_mtime = FETCH_CONFIG_PATH.stat().st_mtime\\n136\\t        if _fetch_config_cache is not None and current_mtime == _fetch_config_mtime:\\n137\\t            return _fetch_config_cache\\n138\\t    except OSError:\\n139\\t        # File doesn't exist or can't be accessed\\n140\\t        return {}\\n141\\t\\n142\\t    # Load the YAML file\\n143\\t    try:\\n144\\t        config = yaml.safe_load(FETCH_CONFIG_PATH.read_text()) or {}\\n145\\t    except OSError:\\n146\\t        return {}  # File doesn't exist\\n147\\t\\n148\\t    # Validate the config structure\\n149\\t    errors = []\\n150\\t\\n151\\t    if \\\"sources\\\" in config:\\n152\\t        if not isinstance(config[\\\"sources\\\"], dict):\\n153\\t            errors.append(\\\"'sources' must be a dictionary\\\")\\n154\\t        else:\\n155\\t            # Validate each source timeout\\n156\\t            for source_name, timeout_config in config[\\\"sources\\\"].items():\\n157\\t                if not isinstance(timeout_config, dict):\\n158\\t                    errors.append(f\\\"sources.{source_name}: must be a dictionary, got {type(timeout_config).__name__}\\\")\\n159\\t                    continue\\n160\\t\\n161\\t                if \\\"timeout_ms\\\" in timeout_config:\\n162\\t                    try:\\n163\\t                        _validate_timeout_ms(timeout_config[\\\"timeout_ms\\\"], f\\\"sources.{source_name}\\\")\\n164\\t                    except FetchConfigValidationError as e:\\n165\\t                        errors.extend(e.errors)\\n166\\t\\n167\\t    if errors:\\n168\\t        raise FetchConfigValidationError(errors)\\n169\\t\\n170\\t    # Project-specific override (if project_slug provided)\\n184\\t    2. Global source-specific timeout\\n185\\t    3. None (use default from spec)\\n186\\t\\n187\\t    Args:\\n188\\t        source: The fetch source to get timeout for\\n189\\t        project_slug: Optional project slug for project-specific overrides\\n190\\t\\n191\\t    Returns:\\n192\\t        Timeout in seconds, or None if not configured\\n193\\t\\n194\\t    Raises:\\n195\\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If config is invalid (on hot-reload)\\n196\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n197\\t    try:\\n198\\t        config = _load_fetch_config()\\n199\\t    except FetchConfigValidationError:\\n200\\t        # On validation error, return None (use default) but log would be useful\\n201\\t        return None\\n202\\t\\n203\\t    source_key = source.value  # Use enum value as key\\n204\\t\\n205\\t    # Check for project-specific override FIRST (if provided)\\n206\\t    if project_slug:\\n207\\t        project_timeouts = config.get(\\\"project_timeouts\\\", {})\\n208\\t        if project_slug in project_timeouts:\\n209\\t            project_config = project_timeouts[project_slug]\\n210\\t            if isinstance(project_config, dict) and source_key in project_config:\\n211\\t                source_override = project_config[source_key]\\n212\\t                if isinstance(source_override, dict) and \\\"timeout_ms\\\" in source_override:\\n213\\t                    timeout_ms = source_override[\\\"timeout_ms\\\"]\\n214\\t                    if timeout_ms is not None:\\n215\\t                        return int(timeout_ms) / 1000\\n216\\t\\n217\\t    # Convert milliseconds to seconds\\n225\\t                return int(timeout_ms) / 1000\\n226\\t\\n227\\t    # No timeout configured\\n228\\t    return None\\n229\\t\\n230\\t\\n231\\tdef get_effective_timeout(spec: FetchCommandSpec, project_slug: str | None = None) -> int | float:\\n232\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n233\\t    Get the effective timeout for a fetch command spec.\\n234\\t\\n235\\t    Priority order:\\n236\\t    1. Project-specific timeout_ms from config file (if project_slug provided and set)\\n237\\t    2. Global timeout_ms from config file (if set)\\n238\\t    3. timeout_seconds from spec (default)\\n239\\t    4. No timeout (infinity) if neither is set\\n240\\t\\n241\\t    Args:\\n242\\t        spec: The fetch command spec\\n243\\t        project_slug: Optional project slug for project-specific overrides\\n244\\t\\n245\\t    Returns:\\n246\\t        Timeout in seconds, or float('inf') for no timeout\\n247\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n248\\t    # Check config file first\\n249\\t    config_timeout = spec.timeout_seconds is not None:\\n255\\t        return spec.timeout_seconds\\n256\\t\\n257\\t    # No timeout - use infinity\\n258\\t    return At startup, `src/main.py` reads it via `tomllib` and passes it to FastAPI — the canvas header badge reads from `/openapi.json` which reflects this. **Do not hardcode the version anywhere else.**\\n50\\t\\n51\\t### Scheme: semantic versioning (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`)\\n52\\t\\n53\\t- `PATCH` — bug fixes, pipeline corrections, prompt tweaks\\n54\\t- `MINOR` — new features, new intent types, new fetch sources, UI additions\\n55\\t- `MAJOR` — architectural changes, breaking API changes\\n56\\t\\n57\\t### Release checklist\\n58\\t\\n59\\t1. Edit `pyproject.toml` — bump `version`\\n60\\t2. Commit: `git commit -m \\\"chore: bump to vX.Y.Z\\\"`\\n61\\t3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`\\n62\\t4. Push: `git push origin main --tags`\\n63\\t\\n64\\tThere is no CI build for adc — it runs directly from source.   Raises:\\n98\\t        FetchConfigValidationError: If the timeout value is invalid\\n99\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n100\\t    if value is None:\\n101\\t        return None\\n102\\t\\n103\\t    # Must be an Convert milliseconds to seconds\\n225\\t                return int(timeout_ms) / 1000\\n226\\t\\n227\\t    # No timeout configured\\n228\\t    return None\\n229\\t\\n230\\t\\n231\\tdef get_effective_timeout(spec: FetchCommandSpec, project_slug: str | None = None) -> int | float:\\n232\\t    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n233\\t    Get the effective timeout for a fetch command spec.\\n234\\t\\n235\\t    Priority order:\\n236\\t    1. Deps live only in the project venv, which is git-ignored and must be recreated after a rebuild. Running `python3 -m uvicorn ...` (the old command) fails with `No module named uvicorn`. Always invoke `.venv/bin/python` (or activate the venv) instead.\\n11\\t\\n12\\t### One-time setup (or after a NixOS rebuild)\\n13\\t\\n14\\t```bash\\n15\\t# From /home/coding/aide-de-camp/\\n16\\tpython3 -m venv .venv\\n17\\t.venv/bin/pip install -e \\\".[dev]\\\"\\n18\\t```\\n19\\t\\n20\\tThis installs fastapi, `uvicorn[standard]`, httpx, websockets, pydantic, aiosqlite (runtime) plus pytest, pytest-asyncio, ruff (dev), and the `src` package itself in editable mode, all from `pyproject.toml`.\\n21\\t\\n22\\tVerify it took:\\n23\\t\\n24\\t```bash\\n25\\t.venv/bin/python -c \\\"import fastapi, uvicorn, httpx, pydantic, aiosqlite; print('deps OK')\\\"\\n26\\t.venv/bin/pytest --collect-only -q   # should collect ~60+ tests\\n27\\t```\\n28\\t\\n29\\t### Start / restart\\n30\\t\\n31\\t```bash\\n32\\t# From /home/coding/aide-de-camp/\\n33\\tnohup .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\\n34\\t\\n35\\t# Restart (kill then start)\\n36\\tkill -2 $(ps aux | grep \\\"uvicorn src.main\\\" | grep -v grep | awk '{print $2}') 2>/dev/null; true\\n37\\tnohup .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 > /tmp/adc.log 2>&1 &\\n38\\t\\n39\\t# Health check (expect HTTP 200 + {\\\"status\\\":\\\"ok\\\",\\\"service\\\":\\\"adc-voice\\\"})\\n40\\tcurl -s http://localhost:8000/health\\n41\\t```\\n42\\t\\n43\\tLogs go to `/tmp/adc.log`. Root logging is configured in `src/main.py` — all `src.*` module loggers are visible at INFO level.\\n44\\t\\n45\\t## Versioning\\n46\\t\\n47\\t**Every meaningful push must bump the version and cut a git tag.**\\n48\\t\\n49\\tVersion lives in `pyproject.toml` → `[project] version`. At startup, `src/main.py` reads it via `tomllib` and passes it to FastAPI — the canvas header badge reads from `/openapi.json` which reflects this. **Do not hardcode the version anywhere else.**\\n50\\t\\n51\\t### Scheme: semantic versioning (`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`)\\n52\\t\\n53\\t- `PATCH` — bug fixes, pipeline corrections, prompt tweaks\\n54\\t- `MINOR` — new features, new intent types, new fetch sources, UI additions\\n55\\t- `MAJOR` — architectural changes, breaking API changes\\n56\\t\\n57\\t### Release checklist\\n58\\t\\n59\\t1. Edit `pyproject.toml` — bump `version`\\n60\\t2. Commit: `git commit -m \\\"chore: bump to vX.Y.Z\\\"`\\n61\\t3. Tag: `git tag vX.Y.Z`\\n62\\t4. Push: `git push origin main --tags`\\n63\\t\\n64\\tThere is no CI build for adc — it runs directly from source. The tag is the release.\\n65\\t\\n66\\t## Architecture\\n67\\t\\n68\\t```\\n69\\tutterance (text or voice)\\n70\\t  → POST /dispatch\\n71\\t  → intent router (ZAI LLM classify)\\n72\\t  → N parallel: fetch strand → synthesize strand\\n73\\t  → persist: topic + result in session.db\\n74\\t  → broadcast SSEEvent(event_type=\\\"result_created\\\", target_surface_id=...)\\n75\\t  → canvas SSE listener calls loadTopics()\\n76\\t  → GET /api/v1/sessions/{session_id}/topics → cards rendered\\n77\\t```\\n78\\t\\n79\\tKey files:\\n80\\t- `src/main.py` — FastAPI app, `/dispatch` endpoint, SSE broadcaster wiring\\n81\\t- `src/intent/router.py` — LLM classification → fetch+synthesize → store persistence\\n82\\t- `src/fetch/commands.py` — fetch command matrix, intent types, data structures\\n83\\t- `src/fetch/orchestrator.py` — concurrent fetch execution with streaming and coverage tracking (FetchStrand implementation)\\n84\\t- `src/synthesize/strand.py` — LLM synthesis into structured result\\n85\\t- `src/session/store.py` — SQLite session store (aiosqlite); `data/session.db`\\n86\\t- `src/sse/broadcaster.py` — SSE connection registry and event routing\\n87\\t- `src/canvas/index.html` — single-page canvas UI\\n88\\t\\n89\\t## ZAI Proxy\\n90\\t\\n91\\tAll LLM calls go through the ZAI proxy at:\\n92\\t\\n93\\t```\\n94\\thttps://zai-proxy-mcp-apexalgo-iad-ts.ardenone.com:8444/v1/messages\\n95\\t```\\n96\\t\\n97\\tSet via `ZAI_PROXY_URL` env var; default is the above. Fix ZeroDivisionError in Options Pipeline\n\n**Priority:** CRITICAL - Active production issue affecting data quality\n\n**Code Solution:**\n```python\ndef calculate_iv(chunk):\n    \"\"\"Calculate implied volatility with proper input validation\"\"\"\n    for idx, row in chunk.iterrows():\n        t = row['T']  # Time to expiration\n        F = row['F']  # Forward price  \n        K = row['K']  # Strike price\n        price = row['undiscounted_option_price']\n        \n        # Pre-calculation validation\n        if t <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid T={t} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if F <= 0 or K <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid price parameters F={F}, K={K} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        if price <= 0:\n            logger.warning(f\"Invalid option price={price} for symbol {row.get('symbol')}, skipping\")\n            continue\n        \n        # Safe calculation with exception handling\n        try:\n            iv = py_vollib_vectorized.implied_volatility.vectorized_implied_volatility(\n                price, F, K, t, flag\n            )\n        except (ZeroDivisionError, ValueError) as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Calculation failed for symbol {row.get('symbol')}: {e}\")\n            continue\n        \n        chunk.at[idx, 'IV'] = iv\n    return chunk\n```\n\n#### 2.
